Operation 'disableBlocking' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_side_effects. Current description is too terse and lacks critical context. AI agents need to understand: what gets disabled (blocking rules/lists), the scope (client-specific vs global), when the change takes effect (immediately), and what parameters control the behavior. Added parameter guidance and side effects for better interpretability.

data.description_suggestion: Disables DNS blocking for the specified client group or globally. When disabled, all DNS queries will be resolved without applying block lists, allow lists, or filtering rules. This operation affects the blocking status immediately. Use the optional client parameter to disable blocking for a specific client group, or omit it to disable blocking globally.
data.reasoning: Current description is too terse and lacks critical context. AI agents need to understand: what gets disabled (blocking rules/lists), the scope (client-specific vs global), when the change takes effect (immediately), and what parameters control the behavior. Added parameter guidance and side effects for better interpretability.

message: Operation 'enableBlocking' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_side_effects. Current description lacks specificity about what 'enabling' means in the blocking context. AI agents need to understand that enabling activates filtering rules and block lists, the scope of the operation, and timing. Added context about rule application and parameter guidance for clarity.

data.description_suggestion: Enables DNS blocking for the specified client group or globally. When enabled, DNS queries will be processed through configured block lists, allow lists, and filtering rules. This operation affects the blocking status immediately. Use the optional client parameter to enable blocking for a specific client group, or omit it to enable blocking globally.
data.reasoning: Current description lacks specificity about what 'enabling' means in the blocking context. AI agents need to understand that enabling activates filtering rules and block lists, the scope of the operation, and timing. Added context about rule application and parameter guidance for clarity.

message: Operation 'blockingStatus' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details. Summary is too generic (noun phrase rather than action). Description lacks information about what the response contains and use cases. Enhanced summary to be action-oriented and added details about the response schema and typical usage patterns for AI agents.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current blocking status for the Blocky instance. Returns a BlockingStatus object containing the enabled/disabled state and any relevant status information. Use this operation to determine whether DNS blocking is currently active before performing blocking control operations.
data.reasoning: Summary is too generic (noun phrase rather than action). Description lacks information about what the response contains and use cases. Enhanced summary to be action-oriented and added details about the response schema and typical usage patterns for AI agents.

message: Operation 'listRefresh' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_side_effects. Current summary and description are too vague about what 'lists' means in the blocking context. AI agents need to understand: which lists are refreshed (block/allow lists), where they come from (external sources), whether the operation is synchronous or asynchronous, and what happens to queries during refresh. Added domain-specific terminology and operational context.

data.summary_suggestion: Refresh all block and allow lists
data.description_suggestion: Triggers a refresh of all configured block lists and allow lists from their external sources. This operation reloads filtering rules including ad-block lists, malware lists, and custom allow/deny lists. The refresh is performed asynchronously and may take time depending on list size and network conditions. DNS queries continue to be processed during the refresh using previously cached list data.
data.reasoning: Current summary and description are too vague about what 'lists' means in the blocking context. AI agents need to understand: which lists are refreshed (block/allow lists), where they come from (external sources), whether the operation is synchronous or asynchronous, and what happens to queries during refresh. Added domain-specific terminology and operational context.

message: Operation 'query' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Description is repetitive and lacks essential details. AI agents need to understand: what the request should contain (domain, parameters), what the response includes (IPs, status, blocking info), and how filtering rules apply. Added schema references and clarification about rule application for better interpretability.

data.description_suggestion: Performs a DNS query and returns the resolution result. Accepts a QueryRequest containing the domain name and optional query parameters. Returns a QueryResult with the resolved IP address(es), response status, and any blocking/filtering information if the query was blocked or affected by configured rules. This operation respects all configured filtering rules, block lists, and client-specific DNS settings.
data.reasoning: Description is repetitive and lacks essential details. AI agents need to understand: what the request should contain (domain, parameters), what the response includes (IPs, status, blocking info), and how filtering rules apply. Added schema references and clarification about rule application for better interpretability.

data.summary_suggestion: Refresh all DNS block and allow lists
data.description_suggestion: Triggers an immediate refresh of all configured DNS block lists and allow lists from their external sources. This operation reloads filtering rules without requiring a service restart. The refresh process may take several seconds depending on list size and network conditions. Use this operation after updating list configurations or to force an immediate update of filtering rules.

data.description_suggestion: Performs a DNS query through Blocky's resolver with full filtering and blocking rules applied. Accepts a QueryRequest containing the domain name and optional query type. Returns a QueryResult with the resolved IP address(es), blocking status, response time, and applied rules. The query is processed through all configured block lists, allow lists, and upstream resolvers according to client group policies.
